w:r.w:t: During the academic year 2024-2028, in partial fulfillment of requirements for award of the degree,
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
grace and blessings that have made this journey possible.
May His guidance continue to illuminate my path in the years ahead.
I am immensely thankful to Dr.
Bindiya M Varghese, head of the department of Computer Science, and Mr.
Ajay Antony Joseph, my Faculty Guide, for their invaluable guidance and timely advice, which played a pivotal role in shaping this project.
Their guidance, constant supervision, and provision of essential information were instrumental in the successful com
I extend my profound thanks to all the professors in the department and the entire staff at RCSS
I am also grateful to my friends and individuals who generously shared their expertise and assistance, contributing significantly to the fulfillment of this endeavor.
student potential through gamification and integrated study tools.
Developed using the Laravel web framework, Mantra provides users with an engaging environment to manage their academic tasks, notes, and schedules interactively.
The platform offers a suite of core features, including a dynamic Chat Interface for real-time collaboration
Additionally, an interactive Calendar mechanism allows users to easily manage new tasks, while the entire system tracks and visually displays total study time and academic milestones.
By combining the social elements of real-time comm
effective digital workspace that encourages continuous learning and collaboration among students.
educational productivity platform designed to foster a
taking, document management, and communication tools
ith powerful gamification elements, Mantra aims to transform the way users interact with their daily academic tasks.
Mantra addresses these challenges by structurally reward
Users earn Experience Points (XP), maintain consecutive activity streaks, and unlock profile achievements as they utilize the platform to study and collaborate.
Developed as a highly responsive web application using the Laravel 12.x PHP framework (operating
diverse frontend is constructed using modern HTML5, CSS3, and vanilla JavaScript for maximum user interactivity.
From an integrated interactive calendar to manage upcoming assignments, to
Notes” system, allowing users to upload documents (such as PDFs and DOCX files) and instantly automatically extract the content into editable, shareable notes.
companion that motivates, organizes, and socially connects learners, Mantra aims to redefine modern academic task management and user engagement.
Building a modern web application like Mantra requires standing on the shoulders of robust, open-source
productivity platform, several key framing documents, architecture guides, and API specifications were deeply analyzed.
This comprehensive guide dictates the structural MVC routing, Eloquent ORM relationships,
To achieve real-time, bidirectional communication for the Chat module without continuous HTTP polling, the RFC 6455 WebSocket protocol specification was studied.
broadcast messages instantly to students collaborating online.
The Smart Notes module necessitates accurately extracting raw text from PDFs and DOCX files.
Literature regarding PHP document processing libraries (such as
decoding proprietary formats, and pushing translated strings into the database relationally.
incorporating gamification. The main modules include:
vated and tracking their academic progress over time.
study metrics, including user achievements, rewards, study streaks, total study time, and Ex
perience Points (XP). By translating time spent studying into quantifiable rewards, it encourages users to stick to their academic tasks.
This module serves as the organizational hub for the student.
It visually lays out assignments, ongoing tasks, and goals on an interactive calendar. Recent
dynamic loading indicator when new tasks are added to prevent UI freezing and confusion.
This module is vital for student socializa
its content to their friends directly within the chat interface.
It also accommodates a music widget to promote focused study sessions.
Users can organize these extracted texts into specific folders, seamlessly transforming static files into editable, shareable Smart Notes.
Working closely with the Gamification Module, this module manages the accurate calculation and visual display of a user’s total active pro
It handles data edge cases naturally;
Business rules define how processes and activities should be conducted to ensure consistency across the application.
to execute successfully through the Smart Notes text extraction engine.
Extracted files must prompt the user with a mandatory folder selection dialog before
being formally instantiated as an editable Note.
Users must be authenticated to actively send and receive messages.
To share notes, the user must own the note,
tasks, creation of notes, and accumulation of total study time.
Study streaks increment purely based on active daily interaction with the core studying components, promoting a strong learning habit.
The Use Case Model identifies the key interactions between the
(the sole actor) and the Mantra platform.
Since there is only a single user type, all use cases revolve around the student’s academic workflow.
The student creates a new account or
and deadlines on the interactive calendar.
The student creates new Smart Notes manually and organizes them into folders.
The student uploads PDF/DOCX files; the sys-
The student selects a note from their library
and shares it inside the chat interface (
XP and streak values are automatically up- dated when the student completes tasks, creates notes,
The student views their accumulated XP, study streaks, total study time, and unlocked achieve-
The following diagram visually represents the interactions between the
actor and the system use cases.
(Model-View- Controller) architecture to guarantee clean, scalable, and secure application code.
The Model layer manages the data relationships inside the PostgreSQL database, ensuring data integrity for things like user notes, milestones, and shared files.
uploaded PDFs and calculating XP based on actions.
To achieve instantaneous message delivery inside the Chat Module,
are utilized to bypass traditional HTTP polling constraints.
File upload streams and extracted note files are managed securely by the Laravel storage
and persists the updated value atomically via Eloquent ORM to prevent race conditions.
The streak counter evaluates whether the student has interacted with any core study compo-
performed server-side using PHP’s Carbon date library to ensure
formatting layers, extracts raw text content, and presents it for folder assignment before persisting to the Notes table. (See Section 4.1.5 for the full design.)
When a student sends a chat message, the Controller persists the message record and then broadcasts an event payload over a dedicated WebSocket channel.
The receiver’s client-side listener dynamically appends the new message element without a page reload.
through specialized open-source PHP libraries (such as
handle real-time concurrency for chat and social integrations seamlessly.
The robust, MVC-driven foundation ensures that introducing new
handled directly via Blade files pai
The economic feasibility of the Mantra platform relies on maximizing impact while
Because the application utilizes open-source resources like PHP 8.2,
Furthermore, WebSocket broadcasting can be managed by affordable, self-hosted solutions or free-tier community APIs.
represents a well-documented and cost-effective approach requiring minimal specialized overhead. As such, Mantra represents an economically sound and highly scalable project.
Mantra demonstrates strong operational feasibility because of its centralized, all-in-one
calendar organization, and progress trackers—into a single unified dashboard, it simplifies a student’s technical overhead.
Gamification strategies systematically reduce friction by offering visual XP incentives and daily streak rewards, creating a recurring retention loop.
PostgreSQL — relational storage for users, notes, messages, and friend re-
Mantra operates with a single user type.
There is no administrative panel or multi-role
; every registered account shares the same privileges and feature acc
The student is the sole actor within the Mantra plat- form.

They register an account, log in securely, and gain full access to all available features. This includes managing tasks and deadlines via the interactive calendar, earning Experience Points (XP) and maintaining daily study streaks through consistent platform engagement, ex- changing
uploading PDF and DOCX documents to be automatically transcribed into editable Smart Notes,
notes into personal folders, and sharing notes with friends directly through the chat. Students own all of their data and collaborate exclusively with other students on the platform.
points, parallel actions, and feedback loops that define the student’s experience on the platform.
dashboard. Core actions such as managing calendar tasks, creating or uploading notes, and chatting with peers

, gamification events (XP awards and streak increments) are triggered automatically as side-effects of productive actions like task completion and note creation, reinforcing the engagement loop without requiring explicit student intervention.
the product has room to grow, avoids architectural bottlenecks, and delivers a robust, responsive feedback loop to the end user.
In Mantra, the system architecture operates via an MVC structure specifically customized to favor engagement.
Unlike standard productivity apps, Mantra implements gamification into its very core.
It balances essential educational utilities with social chat mechanics and an achievement-based XP system designed to make continuous participation rewarding.
unified components such as shared icons (‘
Key features of the UI include:
Clicking on any user’s profile opens a designated view that meticulously presents their accumulated Experience Points (XP), total study time,
The System Architecture diagram maps the communication pathways between the frontend client,
Data storage is engineered relationally via PostgreSQL, emphasizing relational continuity be- tween
Notes & Organization, Social & Chat, and Laravel Framework tables.
streaks, study duration, and user preferences.
Total accumulated active study time in sec-
Manages calendar events with support for recurrence,
Unix timestamp of last session activity (in-
The Class Diagram models the core structural components of Mantra, including the primary entities and their respective responsibilities inside the object-oriented architecture.
It highlights how user authenticated identities correspond relationally to their accumulated notes and chat histories.
user interacts with Mantra’s primary features, particularly focusing on Smart Note extraction and WebSocket message broadcasting
(such as a plain text string or basic document data) is provided into Mantra via the interface,
The framework prompts the user with a customized modal dialog explicitly asking for the
Web application polling is significantly detrimental to large scale chat systems.
Therefore, Mantra’s message pipeline adopts the Publish/Subscribe (
When User A shares a generated Smart Note with User B, the backend Controller commits
spawning the message DOM element without ever having to refresh the active web page.
The Project Pipeline outlines the systematic development workflow followed during the
Identified the core needs of the target user (students) — task management,
gamification. De- fined the functional and non-functional requirements that would guide all subsequent
planned the PostgreSQL relational database schema (Users, Notes, Messages,
tables), and specified the WebSocket integration for real-time chat delivery.
) for the Smart Notes extraction engine.
Configured WebSocket channels for the chat module,
and the Share Note feature between peers.
Executed comprehensive testing across Unit, Integration, System, and
layers to validate individual components, inter-module connectivity, end-to-end system behavior, and load resilience.
Managed all source code changes through Git, maintaining a com-
history of commits, feature branches, and iterative improvements throughout the development lifecycle.
Packaged the Laravel application for production deployment on a Lin-
, storage permissions, and WebSocket services are properly initialized.
esting is a crucial part of web development, ensuring that the application runs smoothly, is secure, and provides a continuous, bug-free experience for users.
Integration testing, System testing, and Performance testing to guarantee the calendar, chat, smart notes, and gamification suites work dependably
Unit testing focuses on evaluating individual components and classes within the Laravel web framework, such as checking if user interactions trigger correct local state changes, or
isolated API logic calculates Gamification XP correctly.
By implementing unit tests via frameworks like
, developers verify stable performance as features scale over time.
study time correctly accumulates and calculates XP
valid textual extraction strings into Note
for Profile XP and study time
Integration testing involves checking how various independent modules of Mantra harmonize and
the Smart Notes module via the folder dialog
Text contents are injected into the PostgreSQL Note database relation-
Ensure that the Share Note modal integrates
correctly with the active WebSocket in- stance payload
interactions automatically trig- ger streak counters upon first login
) ren- der globally across the dashboard
Consistent UI images displayed on the homepage without failing to
System testing evaluates the entire Laravel application contextually as a whole environment, determining if the UI, MVC backend logic, and PostgreSQL queries effectively merge for a usable platform.
Verify that Laravel serves the core landing routing successfully
operate securely behind user authentication gate-
out of reading secure message lines
Check integration of Light/Dark modes against UI colors
Performance testing ensures that Mantra remains stable and scalable during heavy execution times — notably handling
concurrently and extracting intense PDF file data
Response under 4-6 seconds, pre- venting memory
late lifetime total user XP formulas
A clear and organized directory structure is essential for scaling and maintaining the Laravel application. Mantra’s
that controllers, models, views, and public assets are completely decoupled.
Contains all the business logic, including
Houses all the Blade templates used to construct the frontend UI, in-
the Master layouts, Calendar partials, and Chat modals.
: Organizes all the web entry points connecting user requests to the ap- propriate backend Controllers.
The repository for static assets, including
be- fore they are processed by the Smart Notes extraction tools.
To ensure proper version management, seamless collaboration, and reliable distribution, the entire codebase for Mantra is actively maintained using Git version control.
This allows for the efficient tracking of iterations—from early calendar implementations to the later addition of the real-time Chat modules and Smart Note extraction engines.
By pushing updates frequently to GitHub, the development cycle becomes robust against data
application’s progress, guaranteeing that the most stable iteration of the platform is always accessible for final production deployment.

elements. Unlike disconnected applications where students must switch between a calendar app, a note repository, and an instant messenger, Mantra consolidates these workflows into a cohesive, centralized dashboard built on a robust Laravel foundation.
modern remote learning. Concurrently, the Smart Notes module reduces the friction of transcribing or securely

By prioritizing an intuitive user interface utilizing HTML5/CSS3 and smooth, secure backend processes via PHP and Laravel, Mantra delivers a modern, highly effective educational companion that inspires continuous academic growth.
collaboration, several future enhancements are planned to expand the platform’s capabilities:
generate concise summaries or flashcards from the text extracted via the Smart Notes document parser.
Expanding the 1-on-1 WebSocket chat functionality into
Providing users with deeper insights into their pro-
and layouts beyond the standard light/dark modes to further enhance the customized user
rendering complex DOM elements and executing Laravel background jobs smoothly.
: 4 GB (8 GB recommended for heavy WebSocket usage and parsing large PDF doc-
Minimum 10 GB of free space for database storage, caching, and hosting uploaded user document files.
A screen resolution of 1280x720 or higher is recommended for the responsive UI
to display the Calendar, Chat, and Gamification sections optimally.
A stable internet connection is required to communicate with real- time WebSocket broadcasting servers.
